title: What Does Watching Them Age Teach Me?
subtitle: A journal prompt for learning from the ageing you can already see
excerpt: A journal prompt for drawing on the example of an older person whose ageing has been worth watching. The prompt asks what they kept, what they let go, and what their example tells you about what you want for yourself.

practice.whenToUse: Use when thinking about how you want to age and want a concrete example to work from rather than an abstract ideal.

content.text: A ten-minute journal prompt for learning from the ageing you can already observe. Abstract ideals about growing old are hard to hold. Specific examples are easier to work with.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read

Think of one older person you know or have known whose ageing has been worth watching. Someone who handled this chapter in a way that you noticed, positively. They do not need to be famous or exceptional. They need to be specific and real.
What do you see in how they have handled this chapter? What have they kept? What have they let go? What does watching them tell you about what you want for yourself in the decade ahead?

Write without editing. If the example you chose is complicated (someone whose ageing had hard parts alongside the good), write that too. The complicated version is more useful than an uncomplicated ideal.
